animal housing producing Milk, cow, raw (FPCM) in Italy. Additional description: Permanent Meadows Farm - representative of farms located in the Po plain where the cattle were fed principally with forage, compound feed and small quantities of alfalfa and corn feed, without silage feed and protein ingredients. In this system, the forage has spontaneously grown solely in the grassland of the farm and, during warmer seasons, has been left to dry into hay. Therefore, the milk produced is commonly known as “hay milk”, destined to produce Grana Padano cheese as well. Differentiating from the other plain systems, in PMF, meadows were never alternated with other crops, and there was no ploughing or harrowing, only mowing and irrigation. The cattle had the possibility to go in the outdoor paddock during the summer season.. Additional Context: For the given production system, the appropriate allocation method is:System expansion is not necessary, as the system is already representative of a specific agricultural production system (permanent meadows farm). Additional Context: Here is a summary of the relevant life cycle assessment (LCA) information:**System Boundaries:*** The system includes:	+ Animal housing (broiler chicken production)	+ Feed production	+ Vaccines and cleaning production	+ Slaughterhouse	+ Packaging process* Excluded from the system boundaries:	+ Solid-stream processing (outside the slaughterhouse)	+ Production of cleaning agents (outside the slaughterhouse)	+ Composting of manure (outside the system boundaries)	+ Production of animal drugs and chicks (outside the system boundaries)**Functional Unit:*** 1 kg Carcass Weight (CW) is used as the functional unit for the LCA assessment.**Life Cycle Stages:*** Upstream: Chick production and feed production* Downstream: Slaughterhouse, packaging, and distribution**Control Volumes:*** Distinct control volumes for the Grower (who owns and operates the houses) and the Integrator (who owns the birds and feed)* Combined control volume that encompasses the overall costs and life cycle impacts for the entire broiler production system.

animal housing producing Layer chicken, pullet in Mexico. Additional description: Bird development phase (10 weeks). What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: Here is a summary of the relevant life cycle assessment (LCA) information:**System Boundaries:*** The system includes all activities from the chicken farm to the slaughterhouse, including feed production, animal rearing, and packaging.* The system excludes activities outside the slaughterhouse, such as solid-stream processing and the production of capital goods and infrastructures.* The system boundaries include:	+ Chicken farm: production of feed, animal rearing, and production of bedding materials.	+ Slaughterhouse: production of broilers, processing, and packaging.+ Feed production: production of animal feed, including water treatment and wastewater management.	+ Packaging: production of packaging materials and their distribution to the slaughterhouse.**Excluded Activities:*** Solid-stream processing (e.g. processing of feathers, heads, and viscera).* Production of capital goods and infrastructures (e.g. machinery, buildings).* Animal drugs and the production of one-day-old chicks entering the breeder farms.* Composting of manure (although some manure is utilized as fertilizer).**Functional Unit:*** 1 kg Carcass Weight (CW) is used as the functional unit for the LCA assessment.**Inputs and Emissions:***
